title: Seeded Asteroid Generation, Polygons, Sizes, and Difficulty Inputs · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent adaptive-arcade team receives a different viewport, device, frame rate, seed, entity count, accessibility profile, platform, and delivery constraint and transfers seeded asteroid generation, polygons, sizes, and difficulty inputs into a changed seeded readable asteroid factory. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of seeded readable asteroid factory with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-cas…
objectives: Inject a dedicated random generator and record seed and draw order without claiming cryptographic randomness.
Generate ordered angular vertices with bounded radius variation and validate minimum size, winding, and intersections.
Define large, medium, and small radius, speed, score, fragment, rendering, and collision contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Seeded Rng
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Asteroids team handles case 1139 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to generate readable seeded asteroid polygons under geometry, size, spawn, and difficulty budgets; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Inject a dedicated random generator and record seed and draw order without claiming cryptographic randomness.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Seeded Rng.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for inject a dedicated random generator and record seed and draw order without claiming cryptographic randomness. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Seeded Rng, keep Seeded Rng connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

For commit to the mission model, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Difficulty Inputs
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-difficulty-inputs-1vyopa" that uses case 4449 to generate readable seeded asteroid polygons under geometry, size, spawn, and difficulty budgets. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-difficulty-inputs-1vyopa
# Competency: asteroids-difficulty-inputs.
# Case 4449: an offline cross-platform release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects.
# Changed case: cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity.
def evidence_asteroids_difficulty_inputs_1vyopa(vertices: int = 11, min_radius: float = 18.0, max_radius: float = 31.0, seeded_equal: bool = True, self_intersections: int = 0, spawn_budget: int = 7) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1vyopa = "1vyopa-apoyv1-4449"
    assert evidence_variant_1vyopa
    evidence_axes_1vyopa = (
        "player1vyopa",
        "seed4449",
        "inputapoyv1",
        "frame1vyopa4449",
        "state44491vyopa",
        "playtestapoyv14449",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1vyopa)) == 6
    if vertices < 5 or min_radius <= 0.0 or max_radius < min_radius:
        return False, vertices, "polygon-domain"
    if not seeded_equal:
        return False, vertices, "rng-drift"
    if self_intersections > 0:
        return False, self_intersections, "invalid-polygon"
    if spawn_budget < 1:
        return False, spawn_budget, "spawn-budget"
    assert max_radius >= min_radius
    return True, spawn_budget, "seeded-asteroids"

Build deterministic Python evidence to generate readable seeded asteroid polygons under geometry, size, spawn, and difficulty budgets; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates.
